name: Ecom Bot — Slot Memory + Filtered Catalog + Drive Images

parameters.content:
## Ecom Bot — native nodes + Google Drive image send

**Why some nodes are native and some are still HTTP Request:**
- **NocoDB nodes are native** (n8n-nodes-base.nocoDb) — every NocoDB call here uses ONE shared
  platform credential (the master token), so there's no per-client dynamic-secret conflict.
- **OpenRouter and Chatwoot calls stay as HTTP Request, deliberately.** Both need a per-client
  dynamic value (each client's own openrouter_key / chatwoot_token, fetched from their row at
  runtime) plugged into the Authorization header via an expression. n8n's native OpenAI node and
  a hypothetical native Chatwoot node both bind to ONE static credential chosen at build time —
  that doesn't fit this app's per-tenant model, so HTTP Request (which supports fully dynamic
  headers) is the *correct* choice here, not a shortcut.
- **Google Drive download is native** (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) — new in this version, see
  below.

**One-time setup**
1. Credentials -> New -> "NocoDB API Token" credential type (or "Header Auth" with header name
   `xc-token` if your n8n version doesn't have a dedicated NocoDB credential type) — value is
   your NocoDB PAT (nc_pat_...). Never type it into a node directly.
2. Credentials -> New -> Google Drive OAuth2 — one shared account/service account is fine, this
   only needs read access to whatever Drive files store your product images.
3. Open each NocoDB / Google Drive node once and (re)pick your credential + confirm the
   table/operation dropdowns — node parameter names can differ slightly across n8n versions;
   n8n will flag anything that doesn't match after import.
4. ecom_prefs (LongText) must exist on the Leads table — see SETUP.md "Ecom bot memory".

**New in this version — Google Drive product images:** if the single confidently-matched
product's image_url is a Google Drive share link, the workflow downloads the actual image and
sends it as a real WhatsApp image attachment (caption = the bot's reply) instead of pasting the
link as plain text. Any other image host, or no confident single match, falls back to the
previous text-only reply — see the IF node below.

parameters.jsCode:
const d = $input.first().json;
const prompt = `Extract shopping preferences mentioned in this WhatsApp message as STRICT JSON only — no prose, no markdown fences.

Fields (all optional — use null for anything not mentioned in THIS message):
- size (string)
- color (string)
- category (string)
- budget_max (number)
- language (ISO 639-1 code, e.g. "en", "ml", "hi" — detect from the message itself, don't guess from a name)

Message: "${d.messageText}"

Return ONLY a JSON object with exactly these five keys.`;
return [{ json: { ...d, extractionPrompt: prompt } }];

nodes.name: Code · Merge Slots + Resolve Products Table

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { model: $json.client.model || "google/gemini-2.5-flash", temperature: 0.4, messages: [{ role: "system", content: $json.systemPrompt }, { role: "user", content: $json.messageText }] } }}

nodes.name: Code · Extract Reply Text + Detect Drive Image

fieldValues.fieldValue: ={{ JSON.stringify($('Code · Extract Reply Text + Detect Drive Image').first().json.merged) }}
